title: ranunculus repens subsp. reptabundus
intro: Ranunculus repens subsp. reptabundus, commonly known as creeping buttercup, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to reduce swelling and ease pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
